title: kallstroemia solandri
intro: Kallstroemia solandri, also known as the sandpaper bush or desert bird's eye, is a shrubby perennial plant native to Australia and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Kallstroemia solandri include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it can also be used as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
